evals.prompt: Design a cache strategy for an offline-first note-taking app with notes, attachments, and user preferences.
evals.expected_output: The agent maps different cache policies per entity type: cache-first for static assets, network-first for notes, stale-while-revalidate for preferences. Mentions version management.
assertions: The agent assigns different cache strategies per data type
The agent mentions cache and DB version bumping intentionally
The agent addresses attachment/large file caching strategy

evals.prompt: Implement a sync queue for offline writes with conflict resolution.
evals.expected_output: The agent designs a queue with mutation IDs for dedup, exponential backoff on retry, replay on reconnect, and conflict resolution strategy.
assertions: The agent uses mutation IDs for deduplication
The agent implements exponential backoff for retries
The agent describes a conflict resolution strategy (last-write-wins or server-wins)

evals.prompt: Users can still see data after their access is revoked. How do I implement zombie detection?
evals.expected_output: The agent checks permissions before serving cached data, detects revocation, stops reads on revocation, and notifies the user.
assertions: The agent checks permissions before serving cached/offline data
The agent implements revocation detection mechanism
The agent notifies the user when access is revoked

evals.prompt: My service worker is causing memory leaks. How do I diagnose and fix this?
evals.expected_output: The agent checks for listener cleanup on service worker replace/activate, old cache cleanup, and proper event handler management.
assertions: The agent recommends removing event handlers on service worker replacement
The agent mentions cleaning up old caches in the activate handler
The agent addresses the service worker lifecycle

evals.prompt: Design an IndexedDB schema for an offline app with sync progress tracking, annotation storage, and a permission cache.
evals.expected_output: The agent defines separate stores per entity (sync queue, annotations, permissions), includes version number, and addresses sync state persistence.
assertions: The agent defines separate IndexedDB stores for each entity type
The agent includes a version number for the database schema
The agent addresses sync state/progress tracking in the schema
